questions.text: I can work productively even in noisy, unpredictable environments.
I routinely lend my possessions and rarely ask for them back.
I find it difficult to sit back when I have a clear idea of what should be done.
I routinely negotiate for the best possible outcome for myself.
I believe the intensity of my inner life is something most people would find surprising.
I tend to process emotional experiences in the background while staying functionally engaged.
I do not automatically sense the social temperature of a new environment.
I thrive when I have the trust of a group that works together.
I find that the emotional distance I maintain from distressing situations is what enables me to provide help that is both consistent and fair.
I tend to think of my career as the central project of my life.
I believe the most efficient communication is the most literal.
I feel that my job is to support the group in finding its own way.
I hold the view that approaching painful realities with a measured, analytical temperament is the contribution I am best equipped to make and the one I find most meaningful.
I hold the view that a tolerance for mess and ambiguity is a sign of creative flexibility.
My emotional reactions tend to be brief — they register and then dissipate naturally.
I feel that doing things the same proven way each time ensures quality and reliability.
I believe that the moral urgency I experience in the face of suffering is not something I manufacture but something I discover in myself.
I pick up on which topics make certain people visibly tense.
